subject: Update all documentation to reflect modular architecture
body:
Updated documentation files to accurately describe the new modular codebase structure following the January 2026 refactoring from monolithic app.js to 16 specialized modules.

## Changes

### claude.md
- Updated file structure section with complete modular directory tree
- Added new "Modular Architecture" section explaining benefits and organization
- Updated deployment description (ES6 modules instead of single-file)
- Updated tech stack to mention modular architecture
- Revised "Development Workflow" with module-specific guidance
- Updated "Common Tasks" with new file locations for each type of change
- Replaced "Single-File Architecture" limitation with "Modular Architecture" benefits
- Updated performance targets to reflect ES6 module loading
- Enhanced browser compatibility section to mention ES6 module support
- Updated "AI Assistant Guidelines" with module navigation advice
- Added comprehensive "Major Refactoring" entry to recent improvements
- Updated final note to reference modular code locations

### README.md
- Changed description from "single HTML file" to "modular ES6 JavaScript architecture"
- Updated Technology Stack to mention ES6 JavaScript and 16 modules
- Updated Browser Support to note ES6 module requirement
- Fixed line count in changelog: 96% reduction (6,377 → 243 lines)

### FRAMEWORK_ALIGNMENT_PLAN.md
- Added note explaining codebase is now modular
- Updated Migration Checklist with actual module file paths
- Marked all code changes as completed with correct file locations

### QUICK_START_ALIGNMENT.md
- Updated module line counts to match actual codebase
- Removed duplicate app.js entry
- Corrected app.js line count from ~500 to ~243 lines

## Impact
All documentation now accurately reflects:
- 7-directory modular structure
- 16 specialized modules totaling ~6,800 lines
- ES6 module system with import/export
- Dependency injection pattern
- 96% reduction in main orchestrator file size
title: Update all documentation to reflect modular architecture
